name: Soldeu Bike Park and Gall Trail Loop
name_original: Camí de Cap de Rep desde la Cascada de les Moles — circular por el Parque Bici Soldeu
description: This difficult 12.9-mile mountain biking route in Soldeu Bike Park offers challenging terrain, high altitudes, and scenic waterfalls.
tips: Route that connects Pret and the Grandvalira ski resort above Canillo, with multiple gravel slopes to explore in season when the snow disappears. Nice little pond in this natural mountain depression.
A beautiful technical singletrack in the pine forest below the Canillo ski resort in Grandvalira.

A great summer adventure Bike Park located in the Grandvalira area, above Soldeu. The ski center converted into a bike park in the summer is surrounded by multiple trail options that include downhill, freeride, enduro, cross-country and rally at an altitude of 1,800 and 2,450 meters above sea level.
Beautiful forest path through the valley of Valire d'Orient between Canillo and Soldeu. Magnificent views of the valley towards the Cascada de Les Moles.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Camí de Cap de Rep mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for significant elevation changes and technical sections, some of which may require pushing your bike. The technical difficulty is comparable to STS S2, with some parts potentially reaching STS S3-S6.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this 20.7-kilometer circular route is around 2 hours and 50 minutes, depending on your pace and skill level.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, as it's situated in a ski center that transforms into the Soldeu Bike Park during summer. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, lush greenery, and potentially scenic water features like the Cascada de les Moles. The altitude ranges between 1,800 and 2,450 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll pass by the scenic Cascada de les Moles and the beautiful Estany de Cap de Rep. The route also includes sections of the Seig River Trail and the Camí de Gall Forest Trail, offering varied natural beauty.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is it a loop?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area. The trail is accessed from the Soldeu Bike Park, which is part of the Grandvalira area. You can typically find parking and access points near the bike park facilities in Soldeu.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is during the summer months when the Soldeu Bike Park is open. This high-altitude route offers a refreshing experience with lush scenery, making it ideal for summer mountain biking.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Soldeu Bike Park area?
faqs.answer: Yes, as this route is within the Soldeu Bike Park, which is part of the Grandvalira resort, there may be fees or passes required to access the bike park facilities and trails. It's advisable to check the official Grandvalira or Soldeu Bike Park website for current pricing and regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Camí de Cap de Rep trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails in Andorra are dog-friendly, specific regulations can apply within managed areas like the Soldeu Bike Park. It's best to confirm directly with the Grandvalira or Soldeu Bike Park management regarding their pet policies, especially concerning mountain bike trails and lift access.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride parts of El Camí IT1TR5 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer, [CIMA AN09] Port d'Envalira, and multiple segments of the Camí del Gall. It also briefly touches on the Cami de la Costa de les Gerederes and Pont de Collart.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and high altitude, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical terrain, a helmet, plenty of water, and appropriate layers of clothing. Weather can change quickly in the mountains, so be prepared for varying conditions. Basic repair tools and a first-aid kit are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for tackling the challenges of this route?
faqs.answer: Due to the significant elevation changes and technical sections, pacing yourself is key. Don't be afraid to dismount and push your bike through particularly challenging parts, especially if your skills aren't at an advanced level for every segment. Starting early can also help you avoid peak traffic in the bike park and allow ample time to complete the route without rushing.
